seoTitle: Share a Microsoft Teams Meeting Link the Right Way
metaDescription: Copy and send a Microsoft Teams meeting link from an instant Meet Now session or a scheduled calendar event, without confusing guests.
intro: Getting the right people into a Teams call quickly depends on sharing the correct link, whether it's an ad-hoc sync or a scheduled event with attendees. This walkthrough covers grabbing the invite link from both instant and scheduled meetings and getting it into an email or chat.

steps.heading: Start an instant meeting
steps.body: Use the Meet Now option from the calendar panel to launch an on-the-spot session before you've even invited anyone.

steps.heading: Copy the meeting link
steps.body: Select Copy Meeting Link from the meeting screen to save the invitation URL straight to your clipboard.

steps.heading: Or schedule ahead of time
steps.body: For future meetings, fill out the scheduling form with a title, attendee emails, and time range, then save it to generate a shareable link.

steps.heading: Review lobby and access settings
steps.body: Open advanced meeting options to decide who can bypass the lobby, which matters for external guests joining from outside your organization.

steps.heading: Send the link
steps.body: Paste the copied link or full invite details into an email, chat message, or external tool so recipients can join with one click.

faq.question: Do guests need a Microsoft account to use the link?
faq.answer: No, most Teams meeting links let external guests join through a browser without signing in, depending on your organization's settings.

faq.question: Can I share a meeting link before I've scheduled the meeting?
faq.answer: Yes, the Meet Now feature generates a link immediately, even without a calendar entry.

faq.question: What if the link doesn't work for an external guest?
faq.answer: Check your lobby and external access settings, since strict organization policies can block outside participants.

faq.question: Where do I find the link again after the meeting is scheduled?
faq.answer: Open the calendar event and the meeting link, ID, and passcode are listed inside the invite details.
